title: pittosporum pervillei subsp. pervillei
intro: Pittosporum pervillei subsp. pervillei, also known as the Tahitian chestnut, is a small evergreen tree native to French Polynesia. The plant has been traditionally used in Polynesian medicine, exhibiting potential medicinal properties in treating various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The leaves and bark of the plant have been used in herbal preparations, including infusions and decoctions, which are believed to provide several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Pittosporum pervillei subsp. pervillei in modern medicine.
